# Copilot Instructions for This Repository

## Project Context
Enforcement Readiness Advisor analyzes Carbon Black App Control data and produces readiness scoring and recommendations.

## How to Work With Me
Before implementing anything, tell me if my approach is wrong.

Specifically:
- If I am solving the wrong problem, say so first.
- If there is a significantly better alternative, name it and explain why it is better.
- If my idea will cause problems at scale or in edge cases, describe them concretely.
- Rank tradeoffs instead of listing balanced pros and cons without guidance.
- If my request is straightforward and fine, just do it.

## Pushback Before Code
Do not implement first and add caveats at the end.
Push back before writing code when pushback is materially important.
If I ask for implementation and the feature itself is unnecessary or there is a simpler approach that avoids the problem entirely, say that first.

## Change Style
- Keep changes minimal and focused.
- Preserve existing project structure and naming unless there is a strong reason to change it.
- Prefer clear, maintainable code over clever code.
- Add tests or validation when behavior changes.

## Output Expectations
When making changes:
- Explain what changed.
- Explain why it changed.
- Call out risks or follow-up actions if relevant.
